requirements.evidence: Default token mapping lives in android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextField.kt and is asserted by android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextFieldTest.kt; the focused host unit-test command reported PASS.

Multiline textarea behavior is implemented in android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextArea.kt and asserted by android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextAreaTest.kt; the focused host unit-test command reported PASS.

All four InputState border mappings are implemented in android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextField.kt and asserted by android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextFieldTest.kt; the host unit-test command reported PASS.
Focused-state border semantics are exercised by android/app/src/androidTest/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextFieldInstrumentationTest.kt; connectedDebugAndroidTest on Pixel_7_API_34 reported PASS.

Error-state border mapping uses theme.colors.danger.default in android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextField.kt and is asserted by android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextFieldTest.kt; the host unit-test command reported PASS.

Auto-grow and scroll-at-maxRows behavior is implemented in android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextArea.kt and asserted by both android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextAreaTest.kt and android/app/src/androidTest/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextAreaInstrumentationTest.kt; host unit and connected tests reported PASS.

All required textfield and textarea story ids are present in android/app/src/debug/java/com/laneshadow/sandbox/stories/LSInputStories.kt and aggregated from android/app/src/debug/java/com/laneshadow/sandbox/stories/AtomsStories.kt; the host grep gate reported PASS.
android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSTextField.kt and LSTextArea.kt contain no forbidden literal color/font/icon references on inspection, and the host grep gate reported PASS.
The release build completed successfully and the sandbox-symbol gate on android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release-unsigned.apk returned 0 com.nativesandbox entries.

findings.evidence: The new foundation atoms duplicate some token-resolution and semantics behavior that already exists in older ui/components/atoms input components, so future fixes could drift if both stacks remain active.
findings.fix: Plan a follow-up convergence pass once the atom migration settles so legacy input components either delegate to these atoms or are retired.

summary: Approved. The new Android input atoms satisfy the task scope, all targeted host validation passes are green including instrumentation and release APK checks, and I found no merge blocker under the sprint's relaxed review standard. The only residual risk is maintainability drift while the legacy input stack still exists beside these new atoms.
